title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Program Brief: Segmentation Choice
summary: Whitespace splitting treats punctuation as words, a regex drops apostrophes and non-Latin scripts, hyphen policy changes totals silently, and empty tokens enter the frequency table. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the segmentation choice increment in tested word and token contract. Learners progress through…
objectives: Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_tokenization_word_policy_4(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"

# Evidence: bookbot-segmentation-choice-1smzcn
# Competency: bookbot-segmentation-choice.

    assert token_count <= text_length

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Segmentation Choice model · Theory Segmentation Choice, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Segmentation Choice precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Segmentation Choice model · Theory Segmentation Choice, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 2681 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Segmentation Choice model · Theory Segmentation Choice, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One regular expression can correctly tokenize every writing system.
